title: Price Breakdown
description: Breaks down the composition of fixed_price from a list (rate card) price through adjustments. Adjustments fall into three kinds: discounts (change what the buyer pays), commissions (revenue splits that don't affect buyer price), and settlement terms (applied at invoicing). The invariant is: list_price with all discount adjustments applied equals fixed_price. Budgets are always denominated at the fixed_price level, inclusive of commissions.

list_price.description: Rate card or gross price before any adjustments. The starting point from which fixed_price is derived by applying discount adjustments.

adjustments.description: Ordered list of price adjustments. Discount adjustments walk list_price to fixed_price. Commission and settlement adjustments are disclosed for transparency but do not affect the buyer's committed price.

name.description: Specific adjustment name. Use well-known values where applicable for interoperability.

rate.description: Adjustment as a decimal proportion (e.g., 0.15 for 15%). Always positive — kind determines the economic effect. Mutually exclusive with amount.

amount.description: Adjustment as a fixed monetary amount in the pricing option's currency. Always positive — kind determines the economic effect. Mutually exclusive with rate.

description.description: Human-readable description of this adjustment (e.g., 'Malstaffel 12x', '2% Skonto 10 Tage')
